html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Twenty-Three — Part One

The Book of Ezra

EZRA

Covenant History — The Return From Exile

Chapters 1 through 10

YHWH Stirred Up the Spirit of Cyrus — A Scribe Skilled in the Torah — The Hand of YHWH His Creator Was on Him — Even Now There Is Hope for Yisra'EL

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

Orit Ge'ez — Ethiopian Complete Canon

Masoretic Text

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

EZRA — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 10 — THE COVENANT RETURN AND THE COVENANT RESTORATION

EZRA — CHAPTER 1 Cyrus King of Persia — YHWH Stirred Up His Spirit — Whoever Is Among You of All His People — Let Him Go Up — The Vessels of the House of YHWH

1 In the first year of Cyrus king of Persia, that the word of YHWH by the mouth of Yirmeyahu might be fulfilled, YHWH stirred up the spirit of Cyrus king of Persia, so that he made a proclamation throughout all his kingdom and also put it in writing:

2 Thus says Cyrus king of Persia: YHWH, the Creator of heaven, has given me all the kingdoms of the earth, and he has charged me to build him a house at Yerushalayim, which is in Yehudah.

3 Whoever is among you of all his people, may his Creator be with him, and let him go up to Yerushalayim, which is in Yehudah, and rebuild the house of YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL — he is the Creator who is in Yerushalayim.

4 And let each survivor, in whatever place he sojourns, be assisted by the men of his place with silver and gold, with goods and with beasts, besides freewill offerings for the house of Creator that is in Yerushalayim.

5 Then rose up the heads of the fathers' houses of Yehudah and Binyamin, and the priests and the Levites, everyone whose spirit Creator had stirred to go up to rebuild the house of YHWH that is in Yerushalayim.

6 And all who were about them aided them with vessels of silver, with gold, with goods, with beasts, and with costly wares, besides all that was freely offered.

7 Cyrus the king also brought out the vessels of the house of YHWH that Nevukhadnetzar had carried away from Yerushalayim and placed in the house of his gods.

8 Cyrus king of Persia brought these out in the charge of Mithredath the treasurer, who counted them out to Sheshbatzar the prince of Yehudah.

9 And this was the number of them: 30 basins of gold, 1,000 basins of silver, 29 censers,

10 30 bowls of gold, 410 bowls of silver, and 1,000 other vessels.

11 All the vessels of gold and of silver were 5,400. All these did Sheshbatzar bring up, when the exiles were brought up from Babylon to Yerushalayim.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No substantial Ezra manuscript was found at Qumran among the Dead Sea Scrolls, though the book's covenant themes of covenant return, covenant restoration, and covenant Torah renewal align directly with the covenant community's own covenant self-understanding. The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic traditions are the primary authorities for this volume. The covenant historical record of Ezra-Nekhemyah is the covenant narrative of covenant return from Babylonian exile under the covenant permission of the Persian covenant imperial authority.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Ezra Chapter 1 in full. Verse 1 — YHWH stirred up the spirit of Cyrus king of Persia. The covenant action behind the covenant historical event: YHWH stirs the covenant spirit of the pagan covenant king, who then issues the covenant decree that fulfills Yirmeyahu's covenant seventy-year prophecy. The covenant fulfillment of the covenant prophetic word operates through the covenant political decision of a non-covenant ruler whose covenant spirit has been covenant moved by YHWH.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 1 as the foundation of the Ezra narrative. Verse 7-11 — the vessels of the house of YHWH that Nevukhadnetzar had carried away are counted and returned. The covenant vessels carried to Babylon in the covenant judgment — Yirmeyahu 27:22 predicted their return — are now restored under Cyrus's covenant decree. The covenant fulfillment is covenant numerically precise: 5,400 covenant vessels returned.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 2-3 — YHWH, the Creator of heaven, has given me all the kingdoms of the earth, and he has charged me to build him a house at Yerushalayim. The covenant theological claim placed in the mouth of the pagan king: Cyrus acknowledges YHWH as the source of his covenant imperial authority and the commission for the covenant temple restoration. Whether Cyrus understood his own covenant proclamation in these terms is the covenant historical question the text does not answer — but the covenant proclamation itself is covenant documented.

EZRA — CHAPTER 2 The Returned Exiles — The Register of Those Who Came Up — The Priests — The Levites — The Temple Servants — The Sons of Solomon's Servants — Those Who Could Not Prove Their Fathers' Houses — The Total Assembly

1 Now these were the people of the province who came up out of the captivity of those exiles whom Nevukhadnetzar the king of Babylon had carried captive to Babylon. They returned to Yerushalayim and Yehudah, each to his own town.

2 They came with Zerubbavel, Yeshua, Nekhemyah, Serayahu, Re'elyahu, Mordekhai, Bilshan, Mispar, Bigvai, Rekhum, and Ba'anah. The number of the men of the people of Yisra'EL:

3 The sons of Parosh, 2,172.

4 The sons of Shefatyahu, 372.

5 The sons of Arakh, 775.

6 The sons of Pakhath-Moav, namely the sons of Yeshua and Yo'av, 2,812.

7 The sons of Eilam, 1,254.

8 The sons of Zattu, 945.

9 The sons of Zakkai, 760.

10 The sons of Bani, 642.

11 The sons of Bevai, 623.

12 The sons of Azgad, 1,222.

13 The sons of Adonikam, 666.

14 The sons of Bigvai, 2,056.

15 The sons of Adin, 454.

16 The sons of Ater, namely of Hizkiyahu, 98.

17 The sons of Betzai, 323.

18 The sons of Yorah, 112.

19 The sons of Hashum, 223.

20 The sons of Gibbar, 95.

21 The sons of Beit-Lekhem, 123.

22 The men of Netofah, 56.

23 The men of Anatot, 128.

24 The sons of Azmaveth, 42.

25 The sons of Kiryat-Arim, Kefira, and Be'erot, 743.

26 The sons of Ramah and Geva, 621.

27 The men of Mikhmas, 122.

28 The men of Beit-El and Ai, 223.

29 The sons of Nevo, 52.

30 The sons of Magbish, 156.

31 The sons of the other Eilam, 1,254.

32 The sons of Harim, 320.

33 The sons of Lod, Hadid, and Ono, 725.

34 The sons of Yericho, 345.

35 The sons of Sena'ah, 3,630.

36 The priests: the sons of Yedayahu, of the house of Yeshua, 973.

37 The sons of Immer, 1,052.

38 The sons of Pashkhur, 1,247.

39 The sons of Harim, 1,017.

40 The Levites: the sons of Yeshua and Kadmi'el, of the sons of Hodavyahu, 74.

41 The singers: the sons of Asaf, 128.

42 The sons of the gatekeepers: the sons of Shallum, the sons of Ater, the sons of Talmon, the sons of Akkub, the sons of Hatita, and the sons of Shovai, in all 139.

43 The temple servants: the sons of Tziha, the sons of Hasufa, the sons of Tabba'ot,

44 The sons of Keros, the sons of Si'aha, the sons of Padon,

45 The sons of Levana, the sons of Hagava, the sons of Akkuv,

46 The sons of Hagav, the sons of Shamlai, the sons of Hanan,

47 The sons of Giddel, the sons of Gahar, the sons of Re'ayahu,

48 The sons of Retzin, the sons of Nekoda, the sons of Gazzam,

49 The sons of Uzza, the sons of Paseakh, the sons of Besai,

50 The sons of Asnah, the sons of Me'unim, the sons of Nefusim,

51 The sons of Bakbuk, the sons of Hakufa, the sons of Harhur,

52 The sons of Batzluth, the sons of Mehida, the sons of Harsha,

53 The sons of Barkos, the sons of Sisera, the sons of Tamakh,

54 The sons of Netziah, and the sons of Hatipha.

55 The sons of Solomon's servants: the sons of Sotai, the sons of Hassoferet, the sons of Peruda,

56 The sons of Ya'ala, the sons of Darkon, the sons of Giddel,

57 The sons of Shefatyahu, the sons of Hattil, the sons of Pokheret-Hatztzevayim, and the sons of Ami.

58 All the temple servants and the sons of Solomon's servants were 392.

59 The following were those who came up from Tel-Melakh, Tel-Harsha, Keruv, Addon, and Immer, though they could not prove their fathers' houses or their descent, whether they belonged to Yisra'EL:

60 The sons of Delayahu, the sons of Toviyahu, the sons of Nekoda, 652.

61 Also, of the sons of the priests: the sons of Havayah, the sons of Koz, the sons of Barzillai — he had taken a wife from the daughters of Barzillai the Gil'adite, and was called by their name.

62 These sought their registration among those enrolled in the genealogies, but they were not found there, and so they were excluded from the priesthood as unclean.

63 The governor told them that they were not to partake of the most holy food, until there should be a priest to consult Urim and Tummim.

64 The whole assembly together was 42,360,

65 Besides their male and female servants, of whom there were 7,337, and they had 200 male and female singers.

66 Their horses were 736, their mules were 245,

67 Their camels were 435, and their donkeys were 6,720.

68 Some of the heads of families, when they came to the house of YHWH that is in Yerushalayim, made freewill offerings for the house of Creator to erect it on its site.

69 According to their ability they gave to the treasury of the work 61,000 darics of gold, 5,000 minas of silver, and 100 priests' garments.

70 The priests, the Levites, and some of the people lived in Yerushalayim and its vicinity, and the singers, the gatekeepers, and the temple servants lived in their towns, and all Yisra'EL in their towns.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant register of Chapter 2 is the covenant genealogical foundation of the covenant restored community: every covenant family line is documented, every covenant priestly claim verified. The covenant community's covenant integrity is established through covenant genealogical covenant accountability. Those who cannot verify their covenant lineage — verse 59-63 — are excluded from certain covenant functions until covenant clarity is established.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 64 — the whole assembly together was 42,360. The covenant census is the covenant documentary proof that the seventy-year exile produced a covenant viable covenant community for covenant return: 42,360 covenant persons plus servants and singers. The covenant community carried into exile covenant returns as a covenant numbered covenant community.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 2 as the covenant register of the first return. The genealogical list parallels Nekhemyah Chapter 7 with minor variations — the two lists together are the covenant double documentation of the covenant returned community from two covenant perspectives separated by decades. The covenant register is the covenant foundation document of the covenant post-exilic community.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 62-63 — those who could not find their registration in the genealogies were excluded from the priesthood as unclean until a priest could consult Urim and Tummim. The covenant priestly covenant verification mechanism — the Urim and Tummim — is invoked as the ultimate covenant arbiter of covenant priestly legitimacy. The covenant register is important precisely because covenant priestly service requires covenant verified covenant lineage.

EZRA — CHAPTER 3 They Built the Altar — They Kept the Feast of Booths — They Laid the Foundation of the Temple — Many Shouted for Joy — Many Wept With a Loud Voice

1 When the seventh month came, and the children of Yisra'EL were in the towns, the people gathered as one man to Yerushalayim.

2 Then arose Yeshua the son of Yehotzadak, with his fellow priests, and Zerubbavel the son of She'altiel with his kinsmen, and they built the altar of the Creator of Yisra'EL, to offer burnt offerings on it, as it is written in the Torah of Mosheh the man of Creator.

3 They set the altar in its place, for fear was on them because of the peoples of the lands, and they offered burnt offerings on it to YHWH, burnt offerings morning and evening.

4 And they kept the Feast of Booths, as it is written, and offered the daily burnt offerings by number according to the rule, as each day required,

5 And after that the regular burnt offerings, the offerings at the new moon and at all the appointed feasts of YHWH, and the offerings of everyone who made a freewill offering to YHWH.

6 From the first day of the seventh month they began to offer burnt offerings to YHWH. But the foundation of the temple of YHWH was not yet laid.

7 So they gave money to the masons and the carpenters, and food, drink, and oil to the Sidonians and the Tyrians to bring cedar trees from Lebanon to the sea, to Yapho, according to the grant that they had from Cyrus king of Persia.

8 Now in the second year after their coming to the house of Creator at Yerushalayim, in the second month, Zerubbavel the son of She'altiel and Yeshua the son of Yehotzadak made a beginning, together with the rest of their kinsmen, the priests and the Levites and all who had come to Yerushalayim from the captivity. They appointed the Levites, from twenty years old and upward, to supervise the work of the house of YHWH.

9 And Yeshua with his sons and his brothers, and Kadmi'el and his sons, the sons of Yehudah, together supervised the workmen in the house of Creator, along with the sons of Henadad and the Levites, their sons and brothers.

10 And when the builders laid the foundation of the temple of YHWH, the priests in their vestments came forward with trumpets, and the Levites, the sons of Asaf, with cymbals, to praise YHWH, according to the directions of Dawid king of Yisra'EL.

11 And they sang responsively, praising and giving thanks to YHWH, For he is good, for his steadfast love endures forever toward Yisra'EL. And all the people shouted with a great shout when they praised YHWH, because the foundation of the house of YHWH was laid.

12 But many of the priests and Levites and heads of fathers' houses, old men who had seen the first house, wept with a loud voice when they saw the foundation of this house being laid, though many shouted aloud for joy,

13 So that the people could not distinguish the sound of the joyful shout from the sound of the people's weeping, for the people shouted with a great shout, and the sound was heard far away.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant mixed response of Chapter 3 — simultaneous shouting for joy and weeping — is the covenant emotional truth of the covenant restoration: those who had seen the first temple wept at what seemed diminished; those who had only known exile shouted for joy at what had been restored. The covenant community cannot agree on whether the covenant moment is covenant celebratory or covenant sorrowful because it is simultaneously both.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full. Verse 3 — they set the altar in its place, for fear was on them because of the peoples of the lands. The covenant courage of covenant worship in covenant hostile circumstances: the covenant altar is established before the covenant temple foundation is even laid — covenant worship precedes covenant architecture. The covenant community worships before the covenant building is complete because covenant worship is the covenant foundation, not its covenant product.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 3 in full. Verse 11 — for he is good, for his steadfast love endures forever toward Yisra'EL. The covenant refrain from the Tehillim used at the dedication of Solomon's covenant temple — Divrei HaYamim Bet 7:3 — is used again at the laying of the second temple's foundation. The covenant community re-enacts the covenant foundational moment of the first temple in the covenant re-founding of the second.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12-13 — the old men who had seen the first house wept, and the young shouted for joy, and the sounds could not be distinguished. The covenant intergenerational covenant tension: the covenant community is divided not by covenant theology but by covenant memory. Those with covenant memory of what was lost cannot experience the covenant present without covenant grief. Those without that covenant memory can receive the covenant present with pure covenant joy. The covenant community holds both simultaneously.

EZRA — CHAPTER 4 The Adversaries of Yehudah and Binyamin — Let Us Build With You — We Have Nothing to Do With You — They Discouraged the People — The Letter to Artakhshasta — The Work Stopped

1 Now when the adversaries of Yehudah and Binyamin heard that the returned exiles were building a temple to YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL,

2 They approached Zerubbavel and the heads of fathers' houses and said to them, Let us build with you, for we worship your Creator as you do, and we have been sacrificing to him ever since the days of Esarhaddon king of Assyria who brought us here.

3 But Zerubbavel, Yeshua, and the rest of the heads of fathers' houses in Yisra'EL said to them, You have nothing to do with us in building a house to our Creator. But we alone will build to YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, as King Cyrus the king of Persia has commanded us.

4 Then the people of the land discouraged the people of Yehudah and made them afraid to build

5 And hired counselors against them to frustrate their purpose, all the days of Cyrus king of Persia, even until the reign of Darius king of Persia.

6 And in the reign of Akhashverosh, in the beginning of his reign, they wrote an accusation against the inhabitants of Yehudah and Yerushalayim.

7 In the days of Artakhshasta, Bishlam and Mithredath and Tave'el and the rest of their associates wrote to Artakhshasta king of Persia. The letter was written in Aramaic and translated.

8 Rekhum the commander and Shimshai the scribe wrote a letter against Yerushalayim to Artakhshasta the king as follows:

9 Rekhum the commander, Shimshai the scribe, and the rest of their associates, the judges, the governors, the officials, the Persians, the men of Erekh, the Babylonians, the men of Shushan — that is, the Elamites —

10 And the rest of the nations whom the great and noble Osnapper deported and settled in the cities of Samaria and in the rest of the province Beyond the River. And now

11 This is a copy of the letter that they sent: To Artakhshasta the king. Your servants, the men of the province Beyond the River, send greeting. And now

12 Be it known to the king that the Yehudim who came up from you to us have gone to Yerushalayim. They are rebuilding that rebellious and wicked city. They are finishing the walls and repairing the foundations.

13 Now be it known to the king that if this city is rebuilt and the walls finished, they will not pay tribute, custom, or toll, and the royal revenue will be impaired.

14 Now because we eat the salt of the palace and it is not fitting for us to witness the king's dishonor, therefore we send and inform the king,

15 In order that search may be made in the book of the records of your fathers. You will find in the book of the records and learn that this city is a rebellious city, hurtful to kings and provinces, and that sedition was stirred up in it from of old. That was why this city was laid waste.

16 We make known to the king that if this city is rebuilt and its walls finished, you will then have no possession in the province Beyond the River.

17 The king sent an answer: To Rekhum the commander and Shimshai the scribe and the rest of their associates who live in Samaria and in the rest of the province Beyond the River, greeting. And now

18 The letter that you sent to us has been plainly read before me.

19 And I made a decree, and search has been made, and it has been found that this city from of old has risen against kings, and that rebellion and sedition have been made in it.

20 And mighty kings have been over Yerushalayim, who ruled over the whole province Beyond the River, to whom tribute, custom, and toll were paid.

21 Therefore make a decree that these men be made to cease, and that this city be not rebuilt, until a decree is made by me.

22 And take care not to be slack in this matter. Why should damage grow to the hurt of the king?

23 Then, when the copy of King Artakhshasta's letter was read before Rekhum and Shimshai the scribe and their associates, they went in haste to the Yehudim at Yerushalayim and by force and power made them cease.

24 Then the work on the house of Creator that is in Yerushalayim stopped, and it ceased until the second year of the reign of Darius king of Persia.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant opposition to the covenant restoration project is documented in Chapter 4 as covenant systematic and covenant politically sophisticated: the covenant adversaries use the covenant imperial bureaucratic system — letters, official records, royal decrees — as covenant instruments of covenant obstruction. The covenant community faces not merely covenant physical covenant resistance but covenant legal covenant opposition through covenant imperial channels.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full. Verse 2 — let us build with you, for we worship your Creator as you do. The covenant offer that Zerubbavel and Yeshua refuse — verse 3 — is the covenant test of covenant communal covenant integrity: the covenant offer of covenant collaboration with those who practice a covenant hybrid worship is rejected. The covenant community's covenant separateness is the covenant condition of its covenant identity.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 4 in full, switching into Aramaic at verse 8 — the Aramaic section runs through Chapter 6:18. This covenant linguistic shift marks the covenant documentary sections: the official covenant correspondence was in Aramaic, the covenant administrative language of the Persian empire. The covenant text preserves the covenant official documents in their covenant original language.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3 — you have nothing to do with us in building a house to our Creator. The covenant boundary-setting that produces covenant hostility: the covenant returned exiles decline the covenant offer of collaborative covenant worship because they understand their covenant assignment as specifically theirs under the Cyrus decree. The covenant refusal is not covenant arrogance but covenant covenant-boundary maintenance — the covenant temple rebuild is a covenant covenantally defined covenant project.

EZRA — CHAPTER 5 The Prophets Khagai and Zekhariyah Prophesied — Zerubbavel and Yeshua Rose and Began to Rebuild — Who Gave You a Decree to Build? — The Eye of Their Creator Was on the Elders

1 Now the prophets, Khagai and Zekhariyahu the son of Iddo, prophesied to the Yehudim who were in Yehudah and Yerushalayim, in the name of the Creator of Yisra'EL who was over them.

2 Then Zerubbavel the son of She'altiel and Yeshua the son of Yehotzadak arose and began to rebuild the house of Creator that is in Yerushalayim, and the prophets of Creator were with them, supporting them.

3 At the same time Tattenai the governor of the province Beyond the River and Shetar-Bozenai and their associates came to them and spoke to them thus: Who gave you a decree to build this house and to finish this timber work?

4 They also asked them this: What are the names of the men who are building this building?

5 But the eye of their Creator was on the elders of the Yehudim, and they did not stop them until the report should reach Darius and then an answer be returned by letter concerning it.

6 This is a copy of the letter that Tattenai the governor of the province Beyond the River and Shetar-Bozenai and his associates, the inspectors who were in the province Beyond the River, sent to Darius the king.

7 They sent him a report, in which was written as follows: To Darius the king, all peace.

8 Be it known to the king that we went to the province of Yehudah, to the house of the great Creator. It is being built with huge stones, and timber is laid in the walls. This work goes on diligently and prospers in their hands.

9 Then we asked those elders and spoke to them thus: Who gave you a decree to build this house and to finish this timber work?

10 We also asked them their names, for your information, that we might write down the names of their leaders.

11 And this was their reply to us: We are the servants of the Creator of heaven and earth, and we are rebuilding the house that was built many years ago, which a great king of Yisra'EL built and finished.

12 But because our fathers angered the Creator of heaven, he gave them into the hand of Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon, the Chaldean, who destroyed this house and carried away the people to Babylon.

13 However, in the first year of Cyrus king of Babylon, Cyrus the king made a decree that this house of Creator should be rebuilt.

14 And the gold and silver vessels of the house of Creator that Nevukhadnetzar had taken out of the temple that was in Yerushalayim and had brought into the temple of Babylon, these Cyrus the king took out of the temple of Babylon, and they were delivered to one whose name was Sheshbatzar, whom he had made governor.

15 And he said to him, Take these vessels, go and put them in the temple that is in Yerushalayim, and let the house of Creator be rebuilt on its site.

16 Then this Sheshbatzar came and laid the foundations of the house of Creator that is in Yerushalayim, and from that time until now it has been in building, and it is not yet finished.

17 Therefore, if it seems good to the king, let search be made in the royal archives there in Babylon, to see whether a decree was issued by Cyrus the king for the rebuilding of this house of Creator in Yerushalayim. And let the king send us his decision in this matter.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant prophetic renewal of Chapter 5 — Khagai and Zekhariyah prophesied and the covenant leaders rose to rebuild — is the covenant narrative fulfillment of those two covenant prophetic books. The covenant prophecies of Khagai and Zekhariyah were the covenant catalyst that overcame the covenant paralysis of the stopped work: the covenant word of the covenant prophets produced covenant action.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 5 in full. Verse 5 — the eye of their Creator was on the elders of the Yehudim, and they did not stop them. The covenant protection that operates through the covenant political process: the covenant adversarial governor does not stop the covenant work while the covenant matter is referred to Darius. The covenant eye of YHWH prevents the covenant officials from exercising the covenant authority they might otherwise use to stop the covenant building.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 5 in Aramaic from verse 1 onward in the official document sections. Verse 11 — we are the servants of the Creator of heaven and earth, and we are rebuilding the house that was built many years ago. The covenant community's covenant self-identification to the Persian governor: covenant servants of the covenant Creator of the entire cosmos — not merely a regional covenant deity — who are fulfilling a covenant assignment of covenant ancient covenant standing.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12 — because our fathers angered the Creator of heaven, he gave them into the hand of Nevukhadnetzar. The covenant community's official covenant reply to the Persian governor includes a covenant honest covenant theological self-assessment: the exile was covenant judgment for covenant parental covenant sin. The covenant community does not claim covenant innocence — it claims covenant historical covenant accuracy and covenant present covenant authorization under Cyrus's decree.

EZRA — CHAPTER 6 The Decree of Cyrus Was Found — Let the Work on This House of Creator Alone — The Temple Was Finished — The Priests Stood in Their Divisions — They Kept the Feast of Unleavened Bread

1 Then Darius the king made a decree, and search was made in Babylon, in the house of the archives where the documents were stored.

2 And in Ecbatana, the citadel that is in the province of Media, a scroll was found on which this was written: A record.

3 In the first year of Cyrus the king, Cyrus the king issued a decree: Concerning the house of Creator at Yerushalayim, let the house be rebuilt, the place where sacrifices were offered, and let its foundations be retained. Its height shall be 60 cubits and its breadth 60 cubits,

4 With three layers of great stones and one layer of timber. Let the cost be paid from the royal treasury.

5 And also let the gold and silver vessels of the house of Creator, which Nevukhadnetzar took out of the temple that is in Yerushalayim and brought to Babylon, be restored and brought back to the temple that is in Yerushalayim, each to its place. You shall put them in the house of Creator.

6 Now therefore, Tattenai, governor of the province Beyond the River, Shetar-Bozenai, and your associates the inspectors who are in the province Beyond the River, keep away from there.

7 Let the work on this house of Creator alone. Let the governor of the Yehudim and the elders of the Yehudim rebuild this house of Creator on its site.

8 Moreover, I make a decree regarding what you shall do for these elders of the Yehudim for the rebuilding of this house of Creator. The cost is to be paid to these men in full and without delay from the royal revenue, the tribute of the province Beyond the River.

9 And whatever is needed — bulls, rams, or sheep for burnt offerings to the Creator of heaven, wheat, salt, wine, or oil, as the priests at Yerushalayim require — let that be given to them day by day without fail,

10 That they may offer pleasing sacrifices to the Creator of heaven and pray for the life of the king and his sons.

11 Also I make a decree that if anyone alters this edict, a beam shall be pulled out of his house, and he shall be impaled on it, and his house shall be made a dunghill.

12 May the Creator who has caused his name to dwell there overthrow any king or people who shall put out a hand to alter this, or to destroy this house of Creator that is in Yerushalayim. I Darius make a decree. Let it be done with all diligence.

13 Then, according to the word sent by Darius the king, Tattenai, the governor of the province Beyond the River, Shetar-Bozenai, and their associates did with all diligence what Darius the king had ordered.

14 And the elders of the Yehudim built and prospered through the prophesying of Khagai the prophet and Zekhariyah the son of Iddo. They finished their building by decree of the Creator of Yisra'EL and by decree of Cyrus and Darius and Artakhshasta king of Persia.

15 And this house was finished on the third day of the month of Adar, in the sixth year of the reign of Darius the king.

16 And the people of Yisra'EL, the priests and the Levites, and the rest of the returned exiles, celebrated the dedication of this house of Creator with joy.

17 They offered at the dedication of this house of Creator 100 bulls, 200 rams, 400 lambs, and as a sin offering for all Yisra'EL 12 male goats, according to the number of the tribes of Yisra'EL.

18 And they set the priests in their divisions and the Levites in their divisions, for the service of Creator at Yerushalayim, as it is written in the Book of Mosheh.

19 On the fourteenth day of the first month, the returned exiles kept the Passover.

20 For the priests and the Levites had purified themselves together. All of them were clean, so they slaughtered the Passover lamb for all the returned exiles, for their fellow priests, and for themselves.

21 It was eaten by the people of Yisra'EL who had returned from exile, and also by every one who had joined them and separated himself from the uncleanness of the peoples of the land to worship YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL.

22 And they kept the Feast of Unleavened Bread seven days with joy, for YHWH had made them joyful and had turned the heart of the king of Assyria to them, so that he aided them in the work of the house of Creator, the Creator of Yisra'EL.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant finding of the Cyrus decree in Ecbatana — verse 2 — is the covenant historical vindication of the covenant community's covenant claim: the covenant document that authorized the covenant temple restoration existed in the covenant imperial archives exactly as the covenant community claimed. The covenant Persian imperial system becomes the covenant instrument of covenant protection for the covenant returned community.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 6 in full. Verse 21 — every one who had joined them and separated himself from the uncleanness of the peoples of the land to worship YHWH. The covenant universalist inclusion at the covenant Passover: the covenant community's covenant Passover — the covenant foundational covenant feast of covenant exodus — is shared with covenant outsiders who have covenant joined the covenant community and covenant separated themselves from covenant incompatible covenant practices.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text returns to Hebrew at verse 19 after the Aramaic official documents. Verse 22 — YHWH had turned the heart of the king of Assyria to them. The designation of Darius as king of Assyria is the covenant theological statement: the Persian empire is understood in the covenant continuity of Mesopotamian imperial power — Assyria — Babylon — Persia form a covenant continuous covenant political reality under YHWH's covenant sovereign direction.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14 — they finished their building by decree of the Creator of Yisra'EL and by decree of Cyrus and Darius and Artakhshasta king of Persia. The covenant co-authorship of the covenant restoration: the covenant divine decree and the covenant human decrees of three Persian kings are presented as covenant jointly authorizing the covenant temple completion. YHWH's covenant will and Persian covenant imperial authority are presented as covenant converging covenant instruments of the same covenant outcome.

EZRA THE SCRIBE — CHAPTERS 7 THROUGH 10 — THE COVENANT REFORM

EZRA — CHAPTER 7 After These Things in the Reign of Artakhshasta — Ezra Came Up From Babylon — A Scribe Skilled in the Torah of Mosheh — The King Granted Him All He Asked — The Hand of YHWH His Creator Was on Him

1 Now after this, in the reign of Artakhshasta king of Persia, Ezra the son of Serayahu, son of Azaryahu, son of Khilkiyahu,

2 Son of Shallum, son of Tzadok, son of Akhituv,

3 Son of Amaryahu, son of Azaryahu, son of Merayot,

4 Son of Zerakhy ahu, son of Uzzi, son of Bukki,

5 Son of Avishoa, son of Pinekhas, son of El'azar, son of Aharon the chief priest —

6 This Ezra went up from Babylon. He was a scribe skilled in the Torah of Mosheh that YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, had given, and the king granted him all that he asked, for the hand of YHWH his Creator was on him.

7 And there went up also to Yerushalayim, in the seventh year of Artakhshasta the king, some of the people of Yisra'EL, and some of the priests and Levites, the singers and gatekeepers, and the temple servants.

8 And Ezra came to Yerushalayim in the fifth month, which was in the seventh year of the king.

9 For on the first day of the first month he began to go up from Babylon, and on the first day of the fifth month he came to Yerushalayim, for the good hand of his Creator was on him.

10 For Ezra had set his heart to study the Torah of YHWH, and to do it and to teach his statutes and rules in Yisra'EL.

11 This is a copy of the letter that King Artakhshasta gave to Ezra the priest, the scribe, a man learned in matters of the commandments of YHWH and his statutes for Yisra'EL:

12 Artakhshasta, king of kings, to Ezra the priest, the scribe of the Law of the Creator of heaven. Peace. And now

13 I make a decree that anyone of the people of Yisra'EL or their priests or Levites in my kingdom, who freely offers to go to Yerushalayim, may go with you.

14 For you are sent by the king and his seven counselors to make inquiries about Yehudah and Yerushalayim according to the Law of your Creator, which is in your hand,

15 And also to carry the silver and gold that the king and his counselors have freely offered to the Creator of Yisra'EL, whose dwelling is in Yerushalayim,

16 With all the silver and gold that you shall find in the whole province of Babylon, and with the freewill offerings of the people and the priests, vowed willingly for the house of their Creator that is in Yerushalayim.

17 With this money, then, you shall with all diligence buy bulls, rams, and lambs, with their grain offerings and their drink offerings, and you shall offer them on the altar of the house of your Creator that is in Yerushalayim.

18 Whatever seems good to you and your brothers to do with the rest of the silver and gold, you may do, according to the will of your Creator.

19 The vessels that have been given you for the service of the house of your Creator, you shall deliver before the Creator of Yerushalayim.

20 And whatever else is required for the house of your Creator, which it falls to you to provide, you may provide it out of the king's treasury.

21 And I, Artakhshasta the king, make a decree to all the treasurers in the province Beyond the River: Whatever Ezra the priest, the scribe of the Law of the Creator of heaven, requires of you, let it be done with all diligence,

22 Up to 100 talents of silver, 100 cors of wheat, 100 baths of wine, 100 baths of oil, and salt without prescribing how much.

23 Whatever is decreed by the Creator of heaven, let it be done in full for the house of the Creator of heaven, lest his wrath be against the realm of the king and his sons.

24 We also notify you that it shall not be lawful to impose tribute, custom, or toll on anyone of the priests, the Levites, the singers, the doorkeepers, the temple servants, or other servants of this house of Creator.

25 And you, Ezra, according to the wisdom of your Creator that is in your hand, appoint magistrates and judges who may judge all the people in the province Beyond the River, all such as know the laws of your Creator. And those who do not know them, you shall teach.

26 Whoever will not obey the law of your Creator and the law of the king, let judgment be strictly executed on him, whether for death or banishment or confiscation of his goods or imprisonment.

27 Blessed be YHWH, the Creator of our fathers, who put such a thing as this into the heart of the king, to beautify the house of YHWH that is in Yerushalayim,

28 And who extended to me his steadfast love before the king and his counselors, and before all the king's mighty officers. I took courage, for the hand of YHWH my Creator was on me, and I gathered leading men from Yisra'EL to go up with me.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant introduction of Ezra — verse 1-6 — is the covenant priestly genealogy tracing him to Aharon through the Tzadokite covenant line. The covenant scribe skilled in the Torah of Mosheh — verse 6 — is the covenant identity that defines Ezra's covenant mission: covenant textual covenant authority, not merely covenant priestly covenant genealogy. The covenant hand of YHWH on Ezra — verse 6, 9, 28 — is the covenant narrative's covenant explanation of his covenant extraordinary covenant access.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 7 in full. Verse 10 — Ezra had set his heart to study the Torah of YHWH, and to do it and to teach his statutes and rules in Yisra'EL. The covenant triple covenant movement: study — do — teach. The covenant scribe's covenant vocation is the covenant sequence of covenant personal covenant formation followed by covenant communal covenant teaching. The covenant teacher can only teach what the covenant teacher has first studied and covenantally done.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 7 in full with an Aramaic section for the king's letter — verses 12-26 — returning to Hebrew at verse 27. Verse 27-28 — Blessed be YHWH — the covenant doxology that Ezra himself utters upon receiving the king's extraordinary covenant grant. The covenant scribe's first covenant response to covenant providential covenant access is covenant praise of YHWH who moved the king's covenant heart.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 25-26 — appoint magistrates and judges who may judge all the people in the province Beyond the River — all who know the laws of your Creator. And those who do not know them, you shall teach. The covenant dual mandate of Ezra's covenant commission: covenant judicial covenant appointment for those who already know the covenant Torah, and covenant instruction for those who do not. The covenant mission is simultaneously covenant legal reform and covenant educational covenant renewal.

EZRA — CHAPTER 8 The Heads of Families Who Came Up With Ezra — No Levites Were Found — I Was Ashamed to Ask the King for Soldiers — We Fasted and Sought Our Creator — The Hand of Our Creator Was on Us

1 These are the heads of their fathers' houses, and this is the genealogy of those who went up with me from Babylon, in the reign of Artakhshasta the king:

2 Of the sons of Phineas, Gershom. Of the sons of Ithamar, Dani'el. Of the sons of Dawid, Hattush.

3 Of the sons of Shekhaniah, who was of the sons of Parosh, Zekhariah, with whom were registered 150 men.

4 Of the sons of Pakhath-Moav, Eliho'enai the son of Zerahyahu, and with him 200 men.

5 Of the sons of Shekhaniah, the son of Yahazie'el, and with him 300 men.

6 Of the sons of Adin, Eved the son of Yonatan, and with him 50 men.

7 Of the sons of Eilam, Yisha'yahu the son of Atalyahu, and with him 70 men.

8 Of the sons of Shefatyahu, Zevadyahu the son of Mikha'el, and with him 80 men.

9 Of the sons of Yoav, Ovadyahu the son of Yehi'el, and with him 218 men.

10 Of the sons of Shelomit, the son of Yoshifyahu, and with him 160 men.

11 Of the sons of Bevai, Zekhariah the son of Bevai, and with him 28 men.

12 Of the sons of Azgad, Yohanan the son of Hakatan, and with him 110 men.

13 Of the sons of Adonikam, those who came later, their names being Elifelet, Ye'uel, and Shemayahu, and with them 60 men.

14 Of the sons of Bigvai, Utai and Zakkur, and with them 70 men.

15 I gathered them to the river that runs to Ahava, and there we camped three days. As I reviewed the people and the priests, I found there none of the sons of Levi.

16 Then I sent for Eli'ezer, Ari'el, Shemayahu, Elnatan, Yarib, Elnatan, Natan, Zekhariah, and Meshullam, leading men, and for Yoyarib and Elnatan, who were men of insight,

17 And sent them to Iddo, the leading man at the place Kasifya, telling them what to say to Iddo and his brothers and the temple servants at the place Kasifya, namely, to send us ministers for the house of our Creator.

18 And by the good hand of our Creator on us, they brought us a man of discretion, of the sons of Makhli the son of Levi, son of Yisra'EL, namely Sherevyahu with his sons and brothers, 18 men.

19 Also Hashabyahu, and with him Yisha'yahu of the sons of Merari, with his brothers and their sons, 20 men.

20 Besides 220 of the temple servants, whom Dawid and his officials had set apart to attend the Levites. These were all mentioned by name.

21 Then I proclaimed a fast there, at the river Ahava, that we might humble ourselves before our Creator, to seek from him a safe journey for ourselves, our children, and all our goods.

22 For I was ashamed to ask the king for a band of soldiers and horsemen to protect us against the enemy on our way, since we had told the king, The hand of our Creator is for good on all who seek him, and the power of his wrath is against all who forsake him.

23 So we fasted and implored our Creator for this, and he listened to our entreaty.

24 Then I set apart twelve of the leading priests: Sherevyahu, Hashabyahu, and ten of their brothers with them.

25 And I weighed out to them the silver and the gold and the vessels, the offering for the house of our Creator that the king and his counselors and his lords and all Yisra'EL there present had offered.

26 I weighed out into their hand 650 talents of silver, and silver vessels worth 200 talents, and 100 talents of gold,

27 20 bowls of gold worth 1,000 darics, and two vessels of fine bright bronze as precious as gold.

28 And I said to them, You are holy to YHWH, and the vessels are holy, and the silver and the gold are a freewill offering to YHWH, the Creator of your fathers.

29 Guard them and keep them until you weigh them before the chief priests and the Levites and the heads of fathers' houses in Yisra'EL at Yerushalayim, within the chambers of the house of YHWH.

30 So the priests and the Levites took over the silver and the gold and the vessels, to bring them to Yerushalayim, to the house of our Creator.

31 Then we departed from the river Ahava on the twelfth day of the first month, to go to Yerushalayim. The hand of our Creator was on us, and he delivered us from the hand of the enemy and from ambushes by the way.

32 We came to Yerushalayim, and there we remained three days.

33 On the fourth day, within the house of our Creator, the silver and the gold and the vessels were weighed into the hands of Meremot the priest, son of Uriyahu, and with him was El'azar the son of Pinchas, and with them were the Levites, Yozabad the son of Yeshua and No'adyahu the son of Binnui.

34 The whole was counted and weighed, and the weight of everything was recorded.

35 At that time those who had come from captivity, the returned exiles, offered burnt offerings to the Creator of Yisra'EL, 12 bulls for all Yisra'EL, 96 rams, 77 lambs, and as a sin offering 12 male goats. All this was a burnt offering to YHWH.

36 They also delivered the king's commissions to the king's satraps and to the governors of the province Beyond the River, and they aided the people and the house of Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant preparatory fast of Chapter 8 — verse 21-23 — is the covenant decision point of Ezra's covenant leadership: he had publicly declared that YHWH's covenant hand was for good on all who seek him — and he is therefore covenant ashamed to request military escort. The covenant public covenant claim of covenant divine protection constrains the covenant leader from seeking covenant human covenant protection that would contradict the covenant claim.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 8 in full. Verse 22 — I was ashamed to ask the king for soldiers, since we had told the king, The hand of our Creator is for good on all who seek him. The covenant integrity of covenant public covenant testimony: Ezra's covenant theological declaration to the covenant king becomes the covenant constraint on his covenant practical covenant choices. The covenant leader who publicly declares covenant faith in YHWH's covenant protection cannot simultaneously request the covenant king's covenant military protection without covenant contradiction.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 8 in full. Verse 31 — the hand of our Creator was on us, and he delivered us from the hand of the enemy and from ambushes by the way. The covenant vindication of the covenant fast and the covenant faith-declaration: the covenant journey from Ahava to Yerushalayim is accomplished without covenant military escort, and YHWH delivers them from covenant ambush as promised. The covenant faith declared publicly is the covenant faith covenantally fulfilled.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15 — I found there none of the sons of Levi. The covenant problem discovered at the Ahava encampment: the entire covenant returnee company has no Levites. The covenant scribe takes covenant active covenant steps to recruit Levites before departing — verse 16-20 — because the covenant mission requires covenant properly staffed covenant worship. The covenant practical covenant competence of the covenant scribe matches his covenant theological covenant depth.

EZRA — CHAPTER 9 The Officials Approached Ezra — The People of Yisra'EL Have Not Separated Themselves — I Sat Appalled Until the Evening Offering — My Creator I Am Ashamed — We Are Left a Remnant

1 After these things had been done, the officials approached me and said, The people of Yisra'EL and the priests and the Levites have not separated themselves from the peoples of the lands with their abominations, from the Kena'anim, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Yevusites, the Ammonites, the Moabites, the Egyptians, and the Amorites.

2 For they have taken some of their daughters to be wives for themselves and for their sons, so that the holy seed has mixed itself with the peoples of the lands. And in this faithlessness the hand of the officials and chief men has been foremost.

3 As soon as I heard this, I tore my garment and my cloak and pulled hair from my head and beard and sat appalled.

4 Then all who trembled at the words of the Creator of Yisra'EL, because of the faithlessness of the returned exiles, gathered around me while I sat appalled until the evening offering.

5 And at the evening offering I rose from my fasting, with my garment and my cloak torn, and fell upon my knees and spread out my hands to YHWH my Creator,

6 Saying, My Creator, I am ashamed and blush to lift my face to you, my Creator, for our iniquities have risen higher than our heads, and our guilt has mounted up to the heavens.

7 From the days of our fathers to this day we have been in great guilt. And for our iniquities we, our kings, and our priests have been given into the hand of the kings of the lands, to the sword, to captivity, to plundering, and to utter shame, as it is this day.

8 But now for a brief moment favor has been shown by YHWH our Creator, to leave us a remnant and to give us a secure hold within his holy place, that our Creator may brighten our eyes and grant us a little reviving in our slavery.

9 For we are slaves. Yet our Creator has not forsaken us in our slavery, but has extended to us his steadfast love before the kings of Persia, to grant us some reviving to set up the house of our Creator, to repair its ruins, and to give us protection in Yehudah and Yerushalayim.

10 And now, our Creator, what shall we say after this? For we have forsaken your commandments,

11 Which you commanded by your servants the prophets, saying, The land that you are entering, to take possession of it, is a land impure with the impurity of the peoples of the lands, with their abominations that have filled it from end to end with their uncleanness.

12 Therefore do not give your daughters to their sons, neither take their daughters for your sons, and never seek their peace or prosperity, that you may be strong and eat the good of the land and leave it for an inheritance to your children forever.

13 And after all that has come upon us for our evil deeds and for our great guilt, seeing that you, our Creator, have punished us less than our iniquities deserved and have given us such a remnant as this,

14 Shall we break your commandments again and intermarry with the peoples who practice these abominations? Would you not be angry with us until you consumed us, so that there should be no remnant, nor any to escape?

15 O YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, you are just, for we are left a remnant that has escaped, as it is today. Behold, we are before you in our guilt, for none can stand before you because of this.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant prayer of Ezra in Chapter 9 is the covenant model of covenant communal covenant confession: the covenant leader identifies with the covenant community's covenant sin — using we and our throughout — even though the covenant officials have stated that the covenant intermarriage was not committed by Ezra himself. The covenant leader who has not committed the covenant sin confesses it as his own because he is the covenant community's covenant representative.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 9 in full. Verse 8-9 — favor has been shown to leave us a remnant and to give us a secure hold — for we are slaves, yet our Creator has not forsaken us in our slavery. The covenant theology of remnant-in-slavery: the covenant community in Persian bondage — slaves, verse 9 — is simultaneously the covenant community in covenant favor — YHWH has not forsaken them. Covenant slavery and covenant favor coexist in the covenant diaspora condition.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 9 in full. Verse 3 — I tore my garment and my cloak and pulled hair from my head and beard and sat appalled. The covenant physical covenant expression of covenant grief: Ezra's covenant body enacts the covenant community's covenant shame before any covenant words are spoken. The covenant leader's covenant bodily covenant response draws the covenant community of those who trembled at YHWH's words — verse 4 — to gather around him.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13-15 — seeing that you have punished us less than our iniquities deserved — shall we break your commandments again? The covenant logic of the covenant prayer: YHWH's covenant mercy has been greater than covenant justice required, and the covenant community is now about to repeat the covenant violations that produced the covenant exile. The covenant prayer ends not with a covenant solution but with a covenant honest covenant admission: we are before you in our guilt, for none can stand before you because of this.

EZRA — CHAPTER 10 A Very Great Assembly Gathered — Shekhanyah Said We Have Broken Faith — Let It Be Done According to the Torah — All the Men Who Had Married Foreign Women — The Work Was Completed

1 While Ezra prayed and made confession, weeping and casting himself down before the house of Creator, a very great assembly of men, women, and children gathered to him out of Yisra'EL, for the people wept bitterly.

2 And Shekhanyahu the son of Yekhiel, of the sons of Eilam, addressed Ezra: We have broken faith with our Creator and have married foreign women from the peoples of the land, but even now there is hope for Yisra'EL in spite of this.

3 Therefore let us make a covenant with our Creator to put away all these wives and their children, according to the counsel of my lord and of those who tremble at the commandment of our Creator, and let it be done according to the Torah.

4 Arise, for it is your task, and we are with you. Be strong and do it.

5 Then Ezra arose and made the leading priests and Levites and all Yisra'EL take an oath that they would do as had been said. So they took the oath.

6 Then Ezra withdrew from before the house of Creator and went to the chamber of Yehohanan the son of Elyashiv, where he spent the night, neither eating bread nor drinking water, for he was mourning over the faithlessness of the exiles.

7 And a proclamation was made throughout Yehudah and Yerushalayim to all the returned exiles that they should assemble at Yerushalayim,

8 And that if anyone did not come within three days, by order of the officials and the elders all his property should be forfeited, and he himself banned from the congregation of the exiles.

9 Then all the men of Yehudah and Binyamin assembled at Yerushalayim within the three days. It was the ninth month, on the twentieth day of the month. And all the people sat in the open square before the house of Creator, trembling because of this matter and because of the heavy rain.

10 And Ezra the priest stood up and said to them, You have broken faith and married foreign women, and so increased the guilt of Yisra'EL.

11 Now then make confession to YHWH, the Creator of your fathers and do his will. Separate yourselves from the peoples of the land and from the foreign wives.

12 Then all the assembly answered with a loud voice, It is so. We must do as you have said.

13 But the people are many, and it is a time of heavy rain, and we are not able to stand in the open. Nor is this a task for one or two days, for we have greatly transgressed in this matter.

14 Let our officials stand for the whole assembly. Let all in our cities who have taken foreign wives come at appointed times, and with them the elders and judges of every city, until the fierce anger of our Creator over this matter is turned away from us.

15 Only Yonatan the son of Asahel and Yakhzeyahu the son of Tikva opposed this, and Meshullam and Shabbetai the Levite supported them.

16 Then the returned exiles did so. Ezra the priest selected men, heads of fathers' houses, according to their fathers' houses, each of them designated by name. On the first day of the tenth month they sat down to examine the matter.

17 And by the first day of the first month they had come to the end of all the men who had married foreign women.

18 Now there were found some of the sons of the priests who had married foreign women: of the sons of Yeshua the son of Yehotzadak and his brothers: Ma'aseiyahu, Eli'ezer, Yariv, and Gedalyahu.

19 They pledged themselves to put away their wives, and their guilt offering was a ram of the flock for their guilt.

20 Of the sons of Immer: Hanani and Zevadyahu.

21 Of the sons of Harim: Ma'aseiyahu, Eliyahu, Shemayahu, Yekhiel, and Uzziyahu.

22 Of the sons of Pashkhur: Ely o'enai, Ma'aseiyahu, Yishma'el, Netane'el, Yozavad, and Ela'asah.

23 Of the Levites: Yozavad, Shim'i, Kelayahu — that is, Kelita — Petakhyahu, Yehudah, and Eli'ezer.

24 Of the singers: Elyashiv. Of the gatekeepers: Shallum, Telem, and Uri.

25 And of Yisra'EL: of the sons of Parosh: Ramiyahu, Yizziyahu, Malkiyahu, Miyamin, El'azar, Malkiyahu, and Benayahu.

26 Of the sons of Eilam: Mattanyahu, Zekhariyahu, Yekhiel, Avdi, Yeremot, and Eliyahu.

27 Of the sons of Zattu: Ely o'enai, Elyashiv, Mattanyahu, Yeremot, Zavad, and Aziza.

28 Of the sons of Bevai: Yehohanan, Hananyahu, Zabbai, and Athlai.

29 Of the sons of Bani: Meshullam, Mallukh, Adayahu, Yashuv, She'al, and Yeramot.

30 Of the sons of Pakhath-Moav: Adna, Kelal, Benayahu, Ma'aseiyahu, Mattanyahu, Betzal'el, Binnui, and Menasheh.

31 Of the sons of Harim: Eli'ezer, Yishshiyahu, Malkiyahu, Shemayahu, Shim'on,

32 Binyamin, Mallukh, and Shemaryahu.

33 Of the sons of Hashum: Mattenai, Mattattah, Zavad, Elifelet, Yeremai, Menasheh, and Shim'i.

34 Of the sons of Bani: Ma'adai, Amram, Uel,

35 Benayahu, Bedeyahu, Keluhu,

36 Vanyahu, Meremot, Elyashiv,

37 Mattanyahu, Mattenai, Ya'asai,

38 Bani, Binnui, Shim'i,

39 Shelemyahu, Natan, Adayahu,

40 Makhnadebai, Shashai, Sharai,

41 Azarel, Shelemyahu, Shemaryahu,

42 Shallum, Amaryahu, and Yosef.

43 Of the sons of Nevo: Ye'iel, Mattityahu, Zavad, Zevina, Yaddai, Yo'el, and Benayahu.

44 All these had married foreign women, and some of the women had even borne children.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant resolution of Chapter 10 — the covenant community covenant assembly in the heavy rain, the covenant confession, the covenant investigation, the covenant lists — is the covenant institutional response to the covenant crisis identified in Chapter 9. The covenant book of Ezra ends with covenant lists of covenant names, covenant precisely documenting the covenant men who covenant responded to the covenant covenant. The covenant community's covenant integrity is restored through covenant documented covenant accountability.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 10 in full. Verse 2 — Shekhanyahu said, Even now there is hope for Yisra'EL in spite of this. The covenant hope that interrupts the covenant grief: even in the covenant worst covenant moment of covenant communal covenant failure, the covenant leader articulates covenant hope. The covenant confession of covenant sin and the covenant affirmation of covenant hope are not covenant contradictions but covenant companions in the covenant community's covenant life.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 10 in full. Verse 9 — all the people sat in the open square before the house of Creator, trembling because of this matter and because of the heavy rain. The covenant physical discomfort of the covenant assembly — trembling in the covenant rain — is the covenant external expression of the covenant internal covenant gravity of the covenant moment. The covenant community's covenant response to covenant crisis is covenant physically uncomfortable covenant covenant-keeping.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 44 — all these had married foreign women, and some of the women had even borne children. The covenant ending of the book of Ezra with this covenant unresolved covenant complexity: the covenant children born of these covenant unions are not mentioned in any covenant resolution. The covenant book ends with the covenant acknowledgment of the covenant complication without providing the covenant complete covenant resolution. The covenant community's covenant covenant-keeping produces covenant human covenant costs that the covenant text honestly acknowledges.

END OF PART ONE — EZRA
